sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Sociedad are in poor form, winless in their last 7 matches with 3 consecutive losses. Mallorca also struggle, with no wins in their last 5 matches and a tendency to concede first. Both teams have failed to keep clean sheets in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Mallorca have had the upper hand recently, winning both of their last two encounters against Real Sociedad (1-0 and 2-0). Matches between these teams often result in low-scoring affairs, with under 2.5 goals in 9 of their last 10 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Real Sociedad show better possession (55.8%) and attacking presence with high corners per game (7.8), but their conversion rate is poor (20% of big chances). Mallorca mirror similar struggles in attack and defense, conceding slightly more goals (10 vs. 9) this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: Real Sociedad’s home advantage at Reale Arena could play a role, but their inability to score consistently and defensive lapses are concerning. Mallorca’s late goal-scoring tendency (e.g., 30% of goals in 76-90 minutes) might exploit Sociedad’s vulnerabilities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Real Sociedad and Mallorca at the Reale Arena ended in a goalless draw, with neither side able to convert their opportunities. Real Sociedad dominated possession and created more dangerous moments, but Mallorca held firm with a solid defensive structure. Both teams showed moments of intent, though the lack of clinical finishing kept the scoreline level at the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Real Sociedad's attacking dominance
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad clearly controlled the game in the first half, enjoying the lion's share of possession and creating multiple entries into the final third. They generated a couple of big chances but failed to capitalize, often lacking the decisive touch in the penalty area. Their attacking play was relentless at times, with several players contributing to key passes and chance creation. However, their inability to convert high-quality opportunities might be a concern heading into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Mallorca's defensive resilience
halftimeSections.text: Mallorca, on the other hand, adopted a more cautious approach, focusing on defending deep and limiting Real Sociedad's space in dangerous areas. Despite being under pressure for much of the half, they managed to keep a clean sheet with disciplined defending. Their entries into the final third were limited, but they did manage a couple of shots on target, showing some potential to counter when given the chance. Their physicality in duels, especially in the air, helped them disrupt Sociedad's rhythm.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half was heavily tilted in favor of Real Sociedad, with consistent spells of attacking pressure throughout the opening 45 minutes. They started brightly and maintained a strong presence in the opposition half for extended periods, particularly in the early stages and around the midway point of the half. Mallorca had brief moments of attacking intent, especially in a short spell midway through the half, but struggled to sustain any real threat as Sociedad quickly regained control. Towards the end of the half, the game saw more balanced exchanges, though Sociedad still edged the attacking initiative.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Real Sociedad, players like Mikel Oyarzabal and Takefusa Kubo stood out with their involvement in creating chances, and their influence could be crucial in breaking the deadlock. Ander Barrenetxea also showed promise with a shot on target and could be a threat if given more space. For Mallorca, the defensive contributions of players like David López and Martin Valjent were vital in keeping Sociedad at bay, while Mateo Joseph offered a glimpse of attacking potential with a shot on target. Their ability to stay compact will be key in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Real Sociedad will likely continue to push for a breakthrough, potentially introducing fresh legs to maintain their high pressing and attacking intensity. They may look to exploit the flanks more or increase the tempo to catch Mallorca off guard. Mallorca, meanwhile, might stick to their defensive shape but could look to capitalize on counter-attacks if Sociedad overcommit. Their discipline will be tested as fatigue sets in, and they may need to take more risks if they aim to steal a goal.
